name: Whole Wheat Sesame Crackers
recipeInstructions:
1. Position two racks in the center and bottom third of the oven, and preheat to 350°F.
2. Spread 2 tablespoons sesame seeds on each of three un-oiled baking sheets to prevent sticking and achieve a crisp bottom.
3. In a small bowl, combine water, oil, and salt. Stir in half a cup of sesame seeds. Mix to form a rough dough.
4. Transfer the mixture to a lightly floured work surface and knead into a ball.
5. Lightly flour a rolling pin and roll the dough out into a 22-by-14-inch rectangle, about 1/16 inch thick.
6. Prick the dough all over with a fork to release air and keep it flat.
7. Cut the dough into 2-inch squares or other shapes using a pizza wheel or sharp knife, or a 2-inch cookie cutter.
8. Transfer the crackers to prepared baking sheets.
9. Bake until light brown and firm to the touch, about 15-20 minutes, swapping sheet positions halfway through.
10. Cool completely.
(Note: This recipe is adapted from May All Be Fed by John Robbins.)
